timeline.label: Board/CEO review exits Louisiana Clean Energy Complex, Casa Grande green hydrogen, and smaller clean-energy projects; expects up to $2.9B pre-tax charge in fiscal Q3 2026
Q2 FY2026: sales $3.2B (+9% YoY); adjusted EPS $3.20 (+19% vs prior-year quarter); operating recovery after FY2025 charges
FY2025: $3.7B pre-tax business and asset actions (World Energy SAF exit, project exits); GAAP loss $1.74/share; adjusted EPS $12.03
Sold LNG process technology and equipment business; recognized ~$1.6B pre-tax gain in Q4 FY2024
Accelerated clean hydrogen and NEOM green ammonia investments while maintaining core on-site/merchant gases
Follow-on $908M investment in JIGPC joint venture (55% interest) for Middle East industrial gases
Initial $1.6B investment in JIGPC joint venture
NEOM Green Hydrogen Company joint venture formed for Saudi green ammonia facility (VIE consolidation)
Seifi Ghasemi became Chairman, President and CEO; shifted strategy toward energy and environmental markets
Board authorized ongoing share repurchase program (balance used opportunistically)
43 consecutive annual dividend increases through FY2025 ($7.14/share annual)
Company founded; built global industrial gases franchise (on-site, merchant, packaged)

industry.structure: Global industrial gases is an oligopoly (Air Products, Linde, Air Liquide) with high capital barriers, long-lived on-site plants, and contractual pass-through of energy costs. Switching costs are high for large customers.
industry.share_shift: Air Products is reallocating capital away from U.S. clean-energy megaprojects (Louisiana, Casa Grande) back toward core gases; peers compete on pricing discipline and project returns in hydrogen.
industry.trend: Energy transition drives hydrogen and carbon-capture project pipelines, but returns scrutiny has intensified after large impairments; helium merchant remains volatile while non-helium pricing held in FY2025.
